learned_facts.fact: Agent delegation principles: match task complexity to agent capability level; prefer specialist agents over generalists for domain-specific work; provide clear success criteria and constraints, not just task description

Workload balancing: monitor queue depth per agent, redistribute when imbalance exceeds 2:1 ratio; prioritize tasks by urgency and importance (Eisenhower matrix); reserve capacity for urgent unplanned work (20% buffer)

Quality oversight: spot-check agent outputs (sample 10-20% of completed tasks), verify against acceptance criteria, track error rate per agent over time; calibrate review frequency based on agent track record

Escalation management: define clear escalation triggers (timeout, repeated failure, confidence below threshold, out-of-scope request); escalation should include full context (what was tried, what failed, relevant data)

Coordination patterns: sequential handoff (waterfall) for dependent tasks, parallel dispatch for independent tasks, pipeline for streaming work; minimize coordination overhead by giving agents clear interfaces and contracts
